annotations.label: Geometry

anchor.snippet: is a branch of mathematics concerned with properties of space

annotations.note: A description of a field of mathematics, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Theorema Egregium

anchor.snippet: asserts roughly that the Gaussian curvature of a surface is independent

annotations.note: Listed in docs/1000.yaml with no decl; Gaussian curvature and the Theorema Egregium are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume of a frustum (Moscow Papyrus)

anchor.snippet: gives a formula for calculating the volume of a truncated pyramid

annotations.note: No frustum / truncated-pyramid volume formula exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's theorem on diagonals of a cyclic quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: his famous theorem on the diagonals of a cyclic quadrilateral

annotations.note: Only the algebraic Brahmagupta(-Fibonacci) identities exist (Mathlib/Algebra/Ring/Identities.lean); the cyclic-quadrilateral diagonal theorem is absent.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's formula (area of a cyclic quadrilateral)

anchor.snippet: a formula for the area of a cyclic quadrilateral

annotations.note: The cyclic-quadrilateral area formula is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axioms / axiomatic geometry

anchor.snippet: expressing primary or self-evident properties of points, lines, and planes

annotations.note: Mathlib has no synthetic axiomatic (Hilbert/Tarski/Euclid) geometry system.

annotations.label: Point

anchor.snippet: Points are generally considered fundamental objects for building geometry

annotations.note: Mathlib has no primitive 'point' object; points are generic elements of affine/torsor spaces.

annotations.label: Whitehead's point-free geometry

anchor.snippet: Whitehead's point-free geometry , formulated by Alfred North Whitehead

annotations.note: Whitehead's point-free geometry is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Line

anchor.snippet: Euclid described a line as

annotations.note: Lines are represented via affineSpan of two points or AffineMap.lineMap, with no dedicated Line type.

annotations.label: Geodesic

anchor.snippet: a geodesic is a generalization of the notion of a line to curved spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general Riemannian/metric-space geodesic definition.

annotations.label: Plane

anchor.snippet: a plane is a flat, two-dimensional surface that extends infinitely

annotations.note: No dedicated 'plane' object; only general AffineSubspace exists, not a 2-dimensional-plane notion.

annotations.label: Curve

anchor.snippet: A curve is a 1-dimensional object that may be straight

annotations.note: No general 'curve' object, but parametrized/topological curves are formalized as Path / continuous maps.

annotations.label: Curve (topology)

anchor.snippet: a curve is defined by a function from an interval of the real numbers to another space

annotations.note: Path formalizes a continuous map from the unit interval, the special case of a curve defined on [0,1].

annotations.label: Algebraic curve

anchor.snippet: which are defined as algebraic varieties of dimension one

annotations.note: Schemes and Krull/scheme dimension exist (Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme) but there is no dedicated dimension-one algebraic-curve object.

annotations.label: Surface

anchor.snippet: A surface is a two-dimensional object, such as a sphere or paraboloid

annotations.note: No general 'surface' (2-dimensional object) definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solid

anchor.snippet: A solid is a three-dimensional object bounded by a closed surface

annotations.note: No general 'solid' (3-dimensional bounded region) definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Manifold

anchor.snippet: A manifold is a generalization of the concepts of curve and surface

annotations.note: ChartedSpace (and IsManifold) provide the manifold infrastructure generalizing curves and surfaces.

annotations.label: Manifold (topology)

anchor.snippet: a manifold is a topological space where every point has a neighborhood that is homeomorphic to Euclidean space

annotations.note: ChartedSpace formalizes a space locally homeomorphic to a model H, with the Euclidean H giving exactly a topological manifold.

annotations.label: Differentiable manifold

anchor.snippet: a differentiable manifold is a space where each neighborhood is diffeomorphic to Euclidean space

annotations.note: IsManifold formalizes C^n (differentiable) manifolds with corners, generalizing the diffeomorphic-to-Euclidean-space definition.

annotations.label: Angle (Euclid)

anchor.snippet: Euclid defines a plane angle as the inclination to each other

annotations.note: InnerProductGeometry.angle is the undirected angle (inclination) between two vectors/directions.

annotations.label: Angle (modern)

anchor.snippet: an angle is the figure formed by two rays

annotations.note: EuclideanGeometry.angle (∠ p₁ p₂ p₃) is the undirected angle at a vertex formed by two rays.

annotations.label: Length, area, and volume

anchor.snippet: describe the size or extent of an object in one dimension

annotations.note: dist measures length and MeasureTheory.volume measures area/volume, but not as a single unified notion.

annotations.label: Length of a line segment via the Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: the length of a line segment can often be calculated by the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: EuclideanSpace.dist_eq expresses distance as the square root of the sum of squared coordinate differences (the Pythagorean formula).

annotations.label: Area and volume via integrals

anchor.snippet: area and volume can be defined in terms of integrals

annotations.note: Volume is the Lebesgue measure and admits integral representations (e.g. region-between integrals), but is not defined as an integral identity.

annotations.label: Metric

anchor.snippet: The concept of length or distance can be generalized, leading to the idea of metrics

annotations.note: class MetricSpace formalizes a distance function (metric) generalizing length.

annotations.label: Measure theory

anchor.snippet: the concepts of length, area and volume are extended by measure theory

annotations.note: structure Measure is Mathlib's measure-theoretic generalization of length, area, and volume.

annotations.label: Congruence and similarity

anchor.snippet: similarity is used to describe objects that have the same shape

annotations.note: Similar (with Congruent in Mathlib.Topology.MetricSpace.Congruence) formalizes similarity and congruence of point families.

annotations.label: Configuration space of a screw

anchor.snippet: the configuration of a screw can be described by five coordinates

annotations.note: This illustrative configuration-space example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetry determines geometry (Erlangen program)

anchor.snippet: symmetry, expressed via the notion of a transformation group , determines what geometry is

annotations.note: Mathlib has transformation/symmetry groups but does not formalize the Erlangen-program characterization of geometry.

annotations.label: Principle of duality in projective geometry

anchor.snippet: exchange point with plane , join with meet , lies in with contains

annotations.note: Projective space (Projectivization) is formalized, but the point–plane duality principle is not.

annotations.label: Euclidean geometry

anchor.snippet: Euclidean geometry is geometry in its classical sense

annotations.note: EuclideanSpace and the Geometry/Euclidean folder provide Euclidean geometry, though the field is not a single definition.

annotations.label: Differential geometry

anchor.snippet: Differential geometry uses techniques of calculus and linear algebra to study problems in geometry

annotations.note: Manifold and smooth-map infrastructure exists, but 'differential geometry' is a field, not one declaration.

annotations.label: Non-Euclidean geometry

anchor.snippet: Non-Euclidean geometry consists of two geometries based on axioms closely related to those that specify Euclidean geometry

annotations.note: No formalized hyperbolic/elliptic geometry presented as non-Euclidean geometry in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topology

anchor.snippet: Topology is the field concerned with the properties of continuous mappings

annotations.note: class TopologicalSpace and Continuous underlie topology, but the field itself is not a single statement.

annotations.label: Algebraic geometry

anchor.snippet: the study by means of algebraic methods of some geometrical shapes, called algebraic sets

annotations.note: Schemes and zeroLocus (algebraic sets) are formalized, but the field is not one definition.

annotations.label: Hilbert's Nullstellensatz

anchor.snippet: that establishes a strong correspondence between algebraic sets and ideals of polynomial rings

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the Nullstellensatz as vanishingIdeal_zeroLocus_eq_radical.

annotations.label: Complex geometry

anchor.snippet: Complex geometry studies the nature of geometric structures modelled on, or arising out of, the complex plane

annotations.note: Complex analysis and complex manifolds exist piecewise, but 'complex geometry' is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Discrete geometry

anchor.snippet: Discrete geometry is a subject that has close connections with convex geometry

annotations.note: Discrete geometry as a field is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Computational geometry

anchor.snippet: Computational geometry deals with algorithms and their implementations for manipulating geometrical objects

annotations.note: Computational geometry (algorithms on geometric objects) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric group theory

anchor.snippet: Geometric group theory studies group actions on objects that are regarded as geometric

annotations.note: Group actions (MulAction) are formalized, but geometric group theory as a field is not.

annotations.label: Agol's proof of the virtually Haken conjecture

anchor.snippet: Agol's proof of the virtually Haken conjecture that combines Perelman geometrization

annotations.note: The virtually Haken conjecture and its proof are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex geometry

anchor.snippet: Convex geometry investigates convex shapes in the Euclidean space and its more abstract analogues

annotations.note: The Convex predicate and extensive convex-analysis infrastructure exist, but the field is not one statement.

annotations.label: Convexity (Archimedes)

anchor.snippet: Archimedes gave the first known precise definition of convexity

annotations.note: def Convex formalizes convexity via the segment condition, capturing the notion Archimedes defined.
